originalTitle: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
content: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
title: Helping Our Friends in Congo!
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! It's me, your kid-friendly reporter, bringing you important news from our wonderful continent, Africa. Today we are talking about something happening in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's a big country, just like ours, with lots of happy children and families. Sometimes, people can get sick from tiny germs. Right now, some people in Congo are sick with a germ called Ebola. But don't worry, many super smart grown-ups are working very hard to help them. Let's learn about it together!

What is Happening in Congo?

In one part of Congo, some people have gotten sick with a germ called Ebola. This germ can make people feel very unwell. Grown-ups at a special place called the World Health Organization, or WHO, have said this is an "emergency." What does "emergency" mean? It means they want everyone to help quickly! It's like when you see a friend fall, and you rush to help them up. The WHO wants everyone to rush and help the people in Congo. They want to make sure everyone gets better and the germ stops spreading.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is a type of germ. It's so tiny you can't even see it without a special microscope! These tiny germs can get into a person's body and make them feel very sick. People with Ebola might get a fever, which means their body gets very hot. They might also feel tired and have a tummy ache. **Did you know?** The name "Ebola" comes from a river in Congo! This is where the germ was first found a long, long time ago. It's a serious sickness, but doctors and nurses know a lot about how to help people who have it. They give them special medicines and lots of care to help their bodies fight the germ.

How Does Ebola Spread?

This is a very important part! Ebola does not spread through the air, like when someone sneezes and the tiny water drops fly around. You cannot catch Ebola just by being in the same room as someone who is sick, if you are not touching them. Ebola mostly spreads when people touch the body fluids of someone who is very sick. What are body fluids? Things like sweat, spit, or blood. This means you need to be very close to a sick person to catch it. **Did you know?** When doctors and nurses help people with Ebola, they wear special protective suits, gloves, and masks. This helps keep them safe and stops the germ from spreading to them. This is why washing your hands often with soap and clean water is super important! It's one of the best ways to keep all kinds of germs away.

What is the "WHO"?

The "WHO" stands for the World Health Organization. Imagine a super team of doctors, scientists, and health experts from all over the world! Their job is to help everyone on Earth stay healthy. They work in different countries to stop sicknesses. When the WHO says something is an "international emergency," it means they are calling on all countries to help. They want everyone to send doctors, nurses, medicines, and money to help the people in Congo. They are like the biggest health helpers in the whole world! They want every child and every grown-up to be healthy and strong.

Why Did WHO Say "Emergency"?

The WHO said "emergency" because the Ebola germ is still making some people sick in Congo. Even though many smart people are working hard, the germ is still causing problems. There have been around 246 people who got sick and about 80 people who passed away. This makes everyone very sad. When they say "emergency," it's not to make us scared. It's to make us act! It means they want to make sure even more help gets to Congo quickly. They want to stop the germ from spreading to other towns or countries. It's like when your teacher sees someone needs extra help with their homework. They don't want you to be scared, they just want to give more help!

How Are People Helping?

Lots and lots of amazing people are helping right now! * **Doctors and Nurses:** They are working day and night in hospitals to care for sick people. They give them special care to help them get strong again. * **Scientists:** They are studying the germ to find new and better ways to stop it and help people. * **Health Workers:** They are going to different towns to teach people about Ebola. They show them how to wash their hands and how to stay safe. * **Cleaners:** They are cleaning places where sick people have been, to make sure all the germs are gone. * **Governments:** Leaders of countries all over the world are sending money and supplies to help Congo. **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa and around the world are sending their best doctors and nurses to help in Congo. Everyone is working together!

What About Being Scared?

It's okay to feel a little bit worried when you hear about sickness. But it's very important not to be too scared. We have many super brave people working to help. Ebola is mostly in one part of Congo, not everywhere in Africa. The best thing we can do is learn about it, and know how to keep ourselves and our families safe. Being smart about germs is much better than being scared. Remember, washing your hands is a superhero action!

How Can We Stay Safe and Help?

Even if you are far away from Congo, you can still help in many ways! 1. **Wash Your Hands!** Use soap and clean water often, especially before eating and after using the toilet. Sing a happy song while you wash for at least 20 seconds! 2. **Listen to Grown-ups:** Your parents, teachers, and guardians know what is best for you. Listen to their advice about staying healthy. 3. **Eat Healthy Food:** Eating yummy fruits and vegetables helps your body stay strong and fight off germs. 4. **Get Good Sleep:** Rest helps your body recover and stay ready for new adventures! 5. **Be Kind:** If you know someone who is sick, even with a common cold, be kind to them. Sickness is not their fault. **Did you know?** When you wash your hands properly, you are helping to protect not just yourself, but everyone around you too! You are a health hero!

We Are Strong, Africa!

Africa is a continent full of strong, resilient, and caring people. We face challenges, but we always work together to overcome them. The people of Congo are brave, and with help from their friends all over the world, they will get through this. Let's all send our good wishes to the people in Congo. And remember to keep washing those hands and staying healthy! Together, we can make Africa an even healthier and happier place for all children. See you next time, health heroes!
summary: Super doctors are working hard to help sick people in a country called Congo get better from a germ called Ebola, and keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This is important because we want all our friends in Africa to be healthy and happy, and many grown-ups are working together to stop this sickness and teach everyone how to stay safe.

originalTitle: Moment of collision between two Navy jets at Idaho air show
title: Big Planes Had a Sky-Hug at an Air Show! Everyone is Safe!
content:
Hi everyone! Your friendly reporter from Africa here, bringing you some news from far, far away! Today, we’re talking about something that happened in a place called America, at a big, exciting show. Imagine a special party where amazing airplanes come to show off! It’s called an air show.

What is an Air Show?

An air show is like a big, colourful festival for airplanes! People come from all over to watch super-fast jets and big, beautiful planes fly high in the sky. These planes do amazing tricks! They zoom up, they turn quickly, they fly upside down, and sometimes they even make heart shapes with smoke in the air. It’s so much fun to watch! It’s a day full of excitement, looking up at the sky. Pilots are like superheroes who fly these incredible machines. *Did you know?* Some planes at air shows can fly faster than the sound you hear! That means they fly past you before you even hear them coming! Wow!

Meet the Speedy Navy Jets!

The planes we are talking about today are called Navy jets. What are Navy jets? Well, imagine very special, super-fast airplanes that belong to a country's Navy. The Navy usually works with ships on the big ocean, but they also have incredible planes! These jets are built to fly very, very quickly and help keep everyone safe. They are flown by brave pilots who train for many, many years to fly these powerful machines. They are very strong and can do incredible things in the sky. *Did you know?* Navy jets sometimes take off and land on huge ships called aircraft carriers, right in the middle of the ocean! It’s like a floating airport!

A Little Bump in the Sky

Now, something happened at one of these air shows, in a place called Idaho, in America. Two of these amazing Navy jets were flying together, doing their cool tricks. Suddenly, they had a little bump in the sky. Yes, like when two cars accidentally touch each other, but this was high up in the air! It was a moment that made everyone gasp. But here is the most important part: even though the planes touched, no one on the ground was hurt at all. Everyone watching from below was safe.

Our Brave Pilots are Safe!

When the two planes had their bump, the most important thing was to make sure the pilots were okay. And guess what? They are safe! Both pilots are brave and well. How did they get out safely from the planes way up high? They used something very clever called an "ejection seat." Imagine your chair at home, but this chair has a superpower! When there is trouble, the pilot can pull a handle, and *whoosh*! The seat shoots out of the plane, very, very fast! Then, a big, colourful parachute opens up in the sky. The parachute helps the pilot float gently down to the ground, like a feather floating down. It’s a very special and safe way for pilots to get out of their plane if something goes wrong. This amazing safety tool helped both pilots land safely after the bump. We are so happy they are okay! *Did you know?* Pilots train for many, many years to fly these jets. They learn how to be super careful and how to use all the special safety tools, like the ejection seat!

Why Did This Happen?

Sometimes, even when people are very, very careful and super good at their jobs, little accidents can still happen. It's rare, but it can. Think about when you're playing football, and you accidentally bump into a friend. It wasn't on purpose, right? It was just an accident. For these pilots, they practice their tricks thousands of times, but this time, something unexpected happened. It reminds us that even with the best plans and the most amazing machines, sometimes things don’t go exactly as planned. But the good news is, people learn from every accident to make things even safer next time.

Safety is Super Important!

This story also reminds us how important safety is, everywhere! Whether you are playing outside, riding your bicycle, or even watching planes fly, people always try their best to keep everyone safe. After something like this happens, clever people will look very closely at everything. They will try to understand exactly why the planes touched. They do this so they can make sure it doesn't happen again. They will find new ways to make these shows and these planes even safer for everyone. Safety rules are there to protect all of us, and they are always being made better.

Air Shows are Still Amazing!

Even though this little bump happened, air shows are still wonderful events! They are usually very safe and full of amazing sights. Pilots are heroes who show us what incredible things humans and machines can do together. We get to see planes dance in the sky, and it inspires many young boys and girls to dream about flying one day. So, if you ever get a chance to see an air show, remember how much fun they are, and how hard everyone works to keep them safe for all the spectators. It's a fantastic way to see the magic of flight! So, there you have it! A story about super-fast planes, a little bump in the sky, and how everyone stayed safe. It shows us that even when things go a little wrong, people are smart and strong, and they always work hard to make things better and safer. Keep looking up at the sky and dreaming big, my friends!
summary: Two amazing Navy jets had a small bump in the sky during an air show in America, but everyone, especially the brave pilots, is safe and sound!
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even when unexpected things happen, people have clever ways to stay safe, and we can always learn from accidents to make sure everyone is protected.

originalTitle: Fans fury as West Ham on brink of drop - how have they got here?
title: Big Football Team Faces New Challenge!
content:
Hello, Young Football Friends!

Hello, everyone! It's your friend here, reporting live from Africa! Today, we are going to talk about a very exciting game called football, or soccer! Do you love watching football? Do you love cheering for your favorite team? It's so much fun, isn't it? Football is a game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's net. The team that scores more goals wins! It's a game of running, kicking, passing, and working together. It makes your body strong and your heart happy!

A Special Team: West Ham United

There is a football team in a country far away called West Ham United. They wear special jerseys, usually maroon and blue, and play in a big stadium with lots of cheering fans. A few years ago, not too long ago, this team did something super amazing! They won a very, very big and shiny cup! It was a special cup for winning games against other teams from all over Europe. Europe is a continent, just like Africa, but very far away! Winning this cup was like winning the biggest prize in a school competition. Everyone was so happy! The players jumped for joy, and their supporters cheered very, very loudly. It was a wonderful day for West Ham United. **Did you know?** Football is the most popular sport in the world! Millions of people play and watch it every day across many countries, including here in Africa!

The Ups and Downs of Football

You know how sometimes you have a really good day at school, and other times it might be a bit tough? Football is a bit like that too! Some days, teams play wonderfully, score lots of goals, and win. And some days, they try their best, but the ball just doesn't go in, or the other team plays even better. That's okay! It's all part of the game. West Ham United had a super good time when they won that big shiny cup. They practiced hard, worked as a team, and achieved something amazing. That's what makes sports exciting – you never know what will happen!

A New Challenge for West Ham

But now, West Ham United is facing a big challenge. They are playing in a special league called the Premier League. The Premier League is like the top class in school, where all the best teams play. It’s very hard to stay in the Premier League because all the teams are super strong and play wonderfully. This year, West Ham United is finding it a bit tricky. They are not winning as many games as they usually do. Because of this, they might have to move down to a different league next year. This is called "relegation." It just means they would play against different teams in a slightly less famous league. It’s a bit like moving from a super hard class to one that's a little bit easier for a while. **Did you know?** Teamwork is very important in football! If players don't work together, it's very hard to win a game. Just like in school, when you work with your friends, things are easier and more fun!

Why Do Teams Move Up and Down?

Imagine you are playing a game with your friends, and you have different levels. Level 1 is for beginners, and Level 5 is for super players! If you play really well in Level 1, you can move up to Level 2! That's how football leagues work too. If teams win many games, they can move up to a higher, more famous league. And if teams don't win many games, they might move down to a lower league for a little while. It gives every team a fair chance to play at a level that suits them. It's not a bad thing; it just means they get to play against teams that are more like them right now. This helps them get better and stronger!

What Happens When a Team Has a Challenge?

When a team like West Ham United faces a challenge, it means they have to work extra hard. The players and their coach will talk to each other. They will practice even more. They will try to find new ways to play better together. It’s a time to be brave and not give up! Their supporters, the people who love watching them play, will still cheer for them very loudly. They will sing songs and wave flags to show their love. Even if a team is having a tough time, true supporters always believe in them. They know that trying your best is what really matters. **Did you know?** Even the best football players in the world sometimes have tough games. The important thing is to keep trying, keep practicing, and never give up! That's how you learn and grow!

Learning from Challenges

Sometimes, when things are difficult, we learn the most. For West Ham United, this challenging time is a chance to learn, to grow, and to become even stronger for the future. Maybe they will play in the lower league for a year or two, and then they will work their way back up to the Premier League! It's like when you try to learn something new in school, like reading or counting. It might be hard at first, but with practice, you get better and better. And then you feel so proud of yourself! Challenges help us become better versions of ourselves.

Keep Cheering for Your Teams!

So, even if West Ham United is having a tough time, they are still a great team! They are still players who love football and try their best. We should always cheer for teams, whether they are winning or facing challenges. Because football is about joy, friendship, hard work, and never, ever giving up! Keep playing, keep watching, and keep cheering for your favorite teams, my young friends! Every game is a new adventure, and every challenge is a chance to shine!
summary: West Ham United, a football team that recently won a big cup, is now finding it tough to win games and might have to play in a different league next year.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that even famous teams have ups and downs, and it teaches us that working hard and never giving up is always important, no matter the challenge.

content: The decision to allow Matheus Cunha's goal to stand, following an "accidental" handball by Bryan Mbeumo in the build-up, has baffled pundits, players and fans.
